summary.intent: Replace a poorly-implemented bug fix in PR #450 for moltis with a proper solution using an existing Rust crate, fixing UTF-8 corruption/panic bugs in html_to_text() without the O(n²) complexity introduced by the PR.
summary.outcome: Replaced the hand-rolled HTML parser with the html2text crate (0.16.7), reducing the function from ~100 lines to 8, added 4 regression tests covering multibyte scenarios, and created a branch with the committed changes. Cargo.lock was amended into the commit after a hook complication.

repo: The repo has a pre-commit hook that can rebase and revert source file changes, leaving the tree in a detached HEAD state.
Workspace dependencies are defined in the root Cargo.toml under [workspace.dependencies], with a 'Content processing' section around line 237.
CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids .unwrap() in production code.
The only HTML processing dependency prior to this change was the Python html2text package referenced in sandbox packages — no Rust HTML crate was used.

code.finding: html_to_text() used raw byte indexing (bytes[i] as char, &html[i..]) causing UTF-8 corruption and potential panics on non-char-boundary indices for multibyte characters.
html2text = "0.16" added as a dependency after adding to workspace.

workflow: html2text 0.16 returns a Result, requiring either unwrap (forbidden) or a fallback — used unwrap_or_else or similar.
Running cargo test --lib with a filter (e.g., html_to_text) is an effective way to iterate on just the relevant tests before running the full suite.
Cargo.lock must be staged and included in commits when adding new dependencies, or the commit is incomplete.

friction: The pre-commit hook rebased and reverted source file changes, leaving the repo in detached HEAD state and requiring recovery by switching back to the feature branch.
The commit was missing Cargo.lock after the hook interference and required an amend.
Multiple grep attempts were needed to locate existing HTML-related crates in the workspace because the search patterns needed adjustment.
open_items: The PR was created but the transcript ends before confirming it was successfully linked to the original issue #450.
The amended commit including Cargo.lock may not have been pushed — the transcript ends at the amend step without confirming a successful push.
